title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, 37, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer using blue lights and a badge.
Beltran was fired from Lawrence Police in March 2025 after being on leave, later losing peace officer certification in May 2025.
Beltran was reportedly dismissed for incidents involving a discharged gun and inappropriate conduct; he's suing for an alleged phone search.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots are negotiating with the Philadelphia Eagles for wide receiver A.J. Brown.
NFL insider Mike Giardi believes the Eagles will lower their trade demands, making Brown's move to New England likely.
Trading Brown after June 1 would save the Eagles $26.6 million in cap liabilities, influencing their decision.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck fire on the Massachusetts Turnpike in Westborough shut down westbound lanes Thursday morning.
Firefighters from Westborough and Grafton extinguished the blaze by 8:30 a.m.; all lanes reopened by 1:30 p.m.
No injuries were reported, and officials are investigating the cause of the early-morning truck fire.

title: New Patriots WR Romeo Doubs explains why he chose New England in free agency
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romeo Doubs signs a four-year deal with the New England Patriots worth up to $80 million.
Doubs chose the Patriots to team up with quarterback Drake Maye and praised coach Mike Vrabel's winning culture.
Patriots' Eliot Wolf highlighted Doubs' versatility and commitment, noting his early engagement with the playbook.

title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2018 Hyundai Tucson owner faced total engine failure at 60,000 miles, needing a replacement under warranty.
Engine issues in Hyundai and Kia vehicles are linked to machining defects, causing knock and potential failure.
Toyota and Chevrolet are also recalling vehicles due to similar engine bearing problems affecting models like Tundra and GM trucks.

title: After running 19 marathons, he’s making the 2026 Boston Marathon his 20th
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Harry Cicma, 44, will run his 20th marathon at the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Cicma has completed the Boston Marathon previously in 2023.
He runs to support the Stepping Strong Charity and Brigham and Women's Hospital.

title: Is a Stefon Diggs reunion on the table for the Patriots? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stefon Diggs's future with the Patriots is uncertain after being released due to salary cap issues.
Eliot Wolf stated the team is open to considering a return for Diggs to improve the roster.
Diggs posted 1,013 receiving yards in 2025 but struggled during the playoffs with lower productivity.

title: Barnstable man arrested on child porn charges
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Scott Grella, 38, arrested in Barnstable on charges of possession and dissemination of child pornography.
Grella was arrested after a tip led police to his Marstons Mills home; he is held on $3,000 bail.
A pre-trial hearing is scheduled for May 12; Secret Service and State Police assisted in the investigation.

title: Eliot Wolf refutes report that suggested Tom Brady may have kept Patriots from getting Maxx Crosby
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Eliot Wolf dismissed reports suggesting Tom Brady influenced Maxx Crosby's trade away from the Patriots.
Crosby's trade to the Ravens collapsed over concerns about his medical status, leaving him with the Raiders.
Tom Brady, now a minority Raiders owner, refuted having preferences in the ongoing trade decisions.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 12, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Avra Estiatorio, a Greek restaurant from New York, will open in April 2026 at 400 Newbury St., Back Bay.
Celine, inspired by Montreal's culinary scene, opens late summer 2026 at 324 A St., Seaport.
Wonder Food Halls expands in Massachusetts, with six locations open, including Natick and Framingham, and more coming in 2026.
